paragraphs.text: Theophanis the Monk (Volume 3, pp. 66-69) Introductory Note In the Greek Philokalia this poem appears without any introductory note, and nothing is known concerning its author. He lays

particular emphasis upon the need for direct personal experience. Eternal life, he also insists, has to begin here and now, in this present world; but at the same time, like St Gregory of Nyssa, he sees perfection as an endless progress in the age to come, 'a step that has no limit'.
Contents The Ladder of Divine Graces VOLUME 3: Page 67 [V3] 67 Theophanis the Monk
